canonical_definition: A latent property of a prompt that reflects its effectiveness in eliciting meaningful, challenging, and discriminative responses from LLMs, based on criteria such as specificity, domain knowledge, and problem-solving demand.
papers: From Crowdsourced Data to High-quality Benchmarks: Arena-Hard and Benchbuilder Pipeline
R.I.P.: Better Models by Survival of the Fittest Prompts

relationships.paper: R.I.P.: Better Models by Survival of the Fittest Prompts
relationships.evidence: “We employ Llama 3.1-405B-Instruct to measure the quality of prompts on both a binary (useful/not useful) and pointwise scale (0-5).”
